creator: jguojun@sbcglobal.net
text:
Required information is appended below, and I am using binary package via pkg add from 

packages/freebsd.:10:x86:64/All directory.


Just noticed that using startx command without xorg.conf will have X window started with 3 xterms.
But it is not useful due to not accepting mouse and keyboard action because "Allow Empty Input" option seem to be on (True) by default as usual.

This requires a proper xorg.conf in /etc/X11 directory to overwrite "Allow Empty Input" Option.


Also, I found that in all computers I have, the Option "Allow Empty Input"MUST set to "false" to allow
X Window to work.  If this is true for all hardware, what is the reason not to set this by default?

-Jin

________________________________
 From: Jean-Sébastien Pédron <dumbbell@FreeBSD.org>
To: bug-followup@FreeBSD.org; jguojun@sbcglobal.net 
Sent: Saturday, January 18, 2014 4:37 AM
Subject: Re: ports/185182: x11/xorg of 10-RC2 has blank screen on APU-3400 system
 

Hello!

Could you please post:
    o  the output of "dmesg"
    o  the output of "pciconf -lv"

Are you using binary packages (from official FreeBSD pkg(8) repository)
or ports?

If you're using ports, could you post your /etc/make.conf too?

-- 
Jean-Sébastien Pédron
